description: Capstone 01 RAG study corpus. Run: python capstone_01_ingest.py --corpus (skips unchanged). PDF-only here; LangChain API depth lives in web_phase2 + reference/langchain.

tier_guide.1: Start here — LangChain anchor + RAG foundations + survey map
tier_guide.3: Optional stretch — advanced RAG patterns (larger PDFs)

sources.title: LangChain: A framework for building LLM-powered apps (course paper)
sources.why: Only PDF in corpus that is directly about LangChain — high-level, not API docs

sources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP (Lewis et al.)
sources.why: Original RAG paper — what retrieve-then-generate means

sources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey (Gao et al.)
sources.why: One PDF that ties the whole RAG landscape together — good after Lewis

sources.title: Dense Passage Retrieval for Open-Domain QA (Karpukhin et al.)
sources.why: Why Chroma + embeddings beat keyword search

sources.title: Lost in the Middle: How LLMs Use Long Context (Liu et al.)
sources.why: Informs chunk size, k, and what you stuff into RetrievalQA

web_phase2.title: LangChain — Agents (HTML)
web_phase2.why: Official LangChain agents definition — fills gap the course PDF does not cover

web_phase2.title: LangChain — RAG concepts (HTML)
web_phase2.why: Official LangChain mental model for RAG — fills gap the course PDF does not

web_phase2.title: LangChain — Text splitters (HTML)
web_phase2.why: RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter — maps to labs 26–27 and your ingest

web_phase2.title: LangChain — Retrieval (HTML)
web_phase2.why: Retriever, vector store — maps to labs 29–31 and chat script

local_suggestions: Export reference/langchain guide highlights to PDF → capstone/data/pdfs/
Your TRAINING_LOG traps → PDF for personal RAG over mistakes

honest_scope.langchain_depth: Use web_phase2 + playground/reference — corpus PDFs are RAG-weighted
